methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $3.3B - $7.6B.

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $3.33B

data.narrative:
Joby Aviation, a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, is evaluated against a peer universe of three same-sector comparables with similar valuation profiles: Zipline (Series G, $7.6B), Nuro (Series G, $6.0B), and Archer Aviation (Public, $3.3B). This narrower peer set reflects the nascent stage of the autonomous vehicle/drone market and limited public trading comparables in the sector.

The peer-median method, the primary applicable triangulation approach given single-method constraints, yields $6.0B, derived from the median valuation across the three selected peers. The peer range spans $3.3B to $7.6B, with Archer Aviation representing the lower bound and Zipline the upper bound. This method anchors the analysis directly to observed transaction and valuation data for operationally and strategically similar firms.

The triangulated valuation range of $5.1B to $6.9B, with a median of $6.0B, closely aligns with Joby's current market valuation of $6.86B. Key uncertainties include execution risk on advanced air mobility certification and commercialization timelines, which remain unproven at scale across the peer set. The limited public comp availability and early commercial stage of all peers introduce material variance potential in multiples and long-term cash flow assumptions not fully captured in current peer valuations.
